fields.description:
# Layer-2 advisory criteria (judgment) to the coaching spec

## Why
The advisory criteria are where the gate earns its keep — coaching agents to better plans. They must be authored to the feedback spec (located, specific, severity-ranked) and grounded in the DSO-derived checklists, not vague one-liners.

## What
Implement the single-turn, 2-step, and agentic judgment criteria, each as a registry descriptor + prompt/checklist.

## Scope
- Single-turn: F1 measurability, F4 user/problem, E2 ambiguity, E5 testing, G5 decomposition.
- 2-step: E1 coherence, E3 intent-fidelity, E6 verification/termination.
- Agentic (tool-using, codebase-grounded): E4 assumption-probe, G1+G2 edit-set/scope, A1 anti-slop, and the container criteria G3 child-coverage + G4 child-consistency.
- EXP empirical-validation (generic, validated round 4): fires when a plan is complex/novel with an unvalidated assumption AND no spike/probe/measurement planned; SUPPRESSED when experimentation is already present (a fixture/RED test, pilot with metrics, or measurement step).
- Checklists sourced from docs/experiments/data/dso-criteria-specs.md. Each finding follows the coaching spec.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each criterion above is a registry descriptor with exec/facet + a binary checklist + anti-FP rule.
- [ ] Every advisory finding carries criterion id, file/section location, severity, a checklist line, and a suggested fix when confident (validated against the REVIEW_RESULT schema).
- [ ] The EXP criterion fires only when a plan is complex/novel AND lacks an empirical-validation step, and is suppressed when experimentation/spike/measurement is already present (covered by a positive and a negative fixture test).
- [ ] Container criteria (G3/G4) run only when has_children; per the proportionate-scrutiny `applies_at` routing, leaf-grain criteria (E6/G1G2/E4/A1) do not run at epic/story altitude, and TYPE rules apply (bugs exempt; E5 suppressed on RED/GREEN test tasks; F4 N/A on internal leaf tasks).
